title: salix repens var. glabrata
intro: Salix repens var. glabrata, also known as creeping willow, is a variant of the creeping willow plant, a type of shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of salicylic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of Salix repens var. glabrata include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating pain, as well as having antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions and injuries.
